name: cursor-logged-user-indicator
description: Shows your Cursor account in the status bar (Cursor IDE only). Reads cached local session data only; nothing is sent over the network.
author: Federico Seitún

cursorLoggedUser.allowedEmails.description: If non-empty, show a red status bar when the current Cursor email is not in this list (case-insensitive). Merged from User and Workspace settings; when empty, cursorLoggedUser.expectedByFolder is used for the open folder.

cursorLoggedUser.expectedByFolder.markdownDescription: Map of absolute folder paths to `{ "allowedEmails": string[] }`. Used only when merged **cursorLoggedUser.allowedEmails** is empty. The longest matching path prefix of the first workspace folder wins. Example: `"/home/you/work": { "allowedEmails": ["work@corp.com"] }`.

cursorLoggedUser.highlightMatchingAccoun.description: When true and the account satisfies the allow list, emphasize the status item with OK foreground color and a check icon. Custom status bar backgrounds are not supported for extensions (only error/warning background IDs apply), so the bar background stays the default.

colors.description: Foreground when the signed-in email matches the allow list and highlightMatchingAccount is enabled.

scripts.verify: npm run format:check && npm run lint && npm run compile && npm test
